as_user.desc: (Legacy) Pass true to post the message as the authed user. Defaults to true if the chat:write:bot scope is not included. Otherwise, defaults to false.

attachments.desc: A JSON-based array of structured attachments, presented as a URL-encoded string. The `text` field is not enforced as required when using `blocks` or `attachments`.

blocks.desc: A JSON-based array of structured blocks, presented as a URL-encoded string. The usage of the `text` field changes depending on whether you're using `blocks`. The `text` field is not enforced as required when using `blocks` or `attachments`. However, we highly recommended that you include `text` to provide a fallback when using `blocks`, as described above.

channel.desc: Channel, private group, or IM channel to send message to. Can be an encoded ID, or a name.

icon_emoji.desc: Emoji to use as the icon for this message. Overrides `icon_url`.

icon_url.desc: URL to an image to use as the icon for this message.

link_names.desc: Find and link channel names and usernames. You can specify values for `parse` and `link_names` to change formatting behavior.

markdown_text.desc: Accepts message text formatted in markdown. This argument should not be used in conjunction with `blocks` or `text`. Limit this field to 12,000 characters.

parse.desc: Change how messages are treated. Defaults to `none`. See below. _Acceptable values:_ `none` `full` `mrkdwn` `false`. You can specify values for `parse` and `link_names` to change formatting behavior.

text.desc: How this field works and whether it is required depends on other fields you use in your API call. See below for more detail. The usage of the `text` field changes depending on whether you're using `blocks`. The `text` field is not enforced as required when using `blocks` or `attachments`. However, we highly recommended that you include `text` to provide a fallback when using `blocks`, as described above.

thread_ts.desc: Provide another message's `ts` value to post this message in a thread. Avoid using a reply's `ts` value; use its parent's value instead. Ephemeral messages in threads are only shown if there is already an active thread.

user.desc: `id` of the user who will receive the ephemeral message. The user should be in the channel specified by the `channel` argument.

desc: Sends an ephemeral message to a user in a channel.

errors.cannot_reply_to_message: This message type cannot have thread replies.
errors.invalid_attachments: Attachments that contain blocks are not valid
errors.invalid_blocks: Blocks submitted with this message are not valid
errors.invalid_blocks_format: The `blocks` is not a valid JSON object or doesn't match the Block Kit syntax.
errors.markdown_text_conflict: Markdown text cannot be used in conjunction with `blocks` or `text` argument.
errors.message_limit_exceeded: Members on this team are sending too many messages. For more details, see https://slack.com/help/articles/115002422943-Usage-limits-for-free-workspaces
errors.messages_tab_disabled: Messages tab for the app is disabled.
errors.not_in_channel: Cannot post user messages to a channel they are not in.
errors.restricted_action: A workspace preference prevents the authenticated user from posting.
errors.too_many_attachments: Too many attachments were provided with this message. A maximum of 100 attachments are allowed on a message.
errors.user_not_in_channel: Intended recipient is not in the specified channel.
